PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Aug-31-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Henry Jeung in Propria Persona.

Mr. Jeung reported that he spoke with an attorney who reviewed the documents and advised that the only issue was publication and that he has taken care of this issue. The Court that it went through the concerns at the prior appearance. Mr. Jeung stated that the attorney stated that there are no issues and that this case should be settled quickly. The Court stated that is has concerns regarding the next steps, that it thinks it would be helpful for Mr. Jeung to have counsel and stated that if the matter is submitted today it will most likely be denied. Mr. Jeung stated that he is just trying to convert title of the property and would like the matter submitted. The Court stated that it was not sure it had everything it needed. The petition for probate of will and for letters testamentary was taken under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Aug-03-2015 Continued To Aug-31-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Henry Jeung, Petitioner in Pro Per
The Court inquired as to the petitioner's plans to administer the estate. Mr. Jeung responded that the property would be distributed equally by agreement among the beneficiaries. He affirmed that the original will was lodged with the Court. The Court inquired regarding what Mr. Jeung has done to prepare an inventory and accounting. Mr. Jeung's response indicated a lack of understanding of the Court's requirements regarding the necessary filings. The Court advised Mr. Jeung that there are quite a few steps he needs to do to act as administrator of the estate, that the Court would not be granting the Petition as presented, but that he was going to continue the hearing to August 31 and that Mr. Jeung should see if he can get appropriate legal advice from counsel or a qualified paralegal to assist him with the petition before that date. The Petition for Letters of Administration was continued to August 31, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Craig Blackstone/Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not reported)
